rationale: In the Results sections, the authors explicitly report "Spotlight analyses (Spiller et al. 2013)" when probing interaction effects, so the citation is part of their analysis workflow rather than a passing background mention. The cited paper is used to justify how conditional/simple effects are tested and interpreted after moderated regressions. Because the citing study adopts this procedure across multiple studies without extending or disputing it, the role is a substantive application.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s spotlight simple-effects approach to probe significant interaction terms in moderated regression. It uses this method to interpret frame-by-identity and frame-by-construal interaction patterns in its experiments.
